evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implementing testing, monitoring, and data quality checks while partnering with data engineers
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing, monitoring, governance, and metric-definition consistency dominate with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Working with business teams to convert analytical needs into structured data solutions
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors affect retail/ecommerce business decisions without regulatory or financial-reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: owning the analytics layer of our data platform and bridging data engineering with business intelligence
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A distinct data engineering function and an established platform with self-serve analytics ambitions point to mid maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: Building robust dbt models that translate business logic into scalable solutions while optimizing for performance and cost efficiency
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tools (dbt, Looker Studio, Omni) and specific modeling concepts (facts, dimensions, grains) show what the role does day to day.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Enabling governed access to trusted data sources and optimizing the platform for AI use cases
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data platform and its consumers — self-serve analytics and AI use cases — not a specific commercial function.
evidence.autonomy_level: owning the analytics layer of our data platform; Ability to own cross-functional analytics problems end-to-end
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Full ownership language over the analytics layer and end-to-end problem ownership is unambiguously strategic.
evidence.ai_role: Openness to using AI tools for workflow improvement; optimizing the platform for AI use cases
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Both signals present — using AI tools personally and building AI-ready infrastructure — with the personal-use signal being the clearer requirement.
evidence.testing_framing: Implementing testing, monitoring, and data quality checks
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality checks are framed as an owned implementation responsibility, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Enabling governed access to trusted data sources
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: governed access and data quality checks are a concern but secondary to platform-building and stakeholder delivery.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Building robust dbt models that translate business logic into scalable solutions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new self-serve analytics capability and optimizing existing dbt models for performance and cost.
